text:
The system was outputting decent, well-behaved people practising defensive medicine.

Careful. Cautious. Technically correct. Ordered the right tests. Followed the right protocols. Never too far from the guideline.

And I understood why. When you have never had a sandbox, caution is the only tool you have. You do not trust your hands because your hands have never been tested. So you order more. You consult more. You hedge more.

But this is a human life.

Not a board exam. Not a performance review. A person in a room who needs someone to think clearly and move with conviction.

Defensive medicine keeps you safe. It does not always keep the patient safe.

I did not want to be careful in the wrong way anymore.
